title: Seizure (Epileptic Fit)
immediate_action: Protect from injury. Do NOT restrain. Time the seizure.

steps.instruction: Clear the area around the person. Move furniture, sharp objects, and anything dangerous away.
steps.tip: Protect the head — place something soft under it like a folded jacket.

steps.instruction: TIME the seizure. Start a timer NOW. Call emergency if it lasts more than 5 minutes.
steps.tip: Seizures usually last 1-3 minutes. Over 5 minutes = status epilepticus = emergency.

steps.instruction: Do NOT restrain them. Do NOT put anything in their mouth. Just protect from injury.
steps.tip: They cannot swallow their tongue — this is a myth. Putting objects in mouth causes injury.

steps.instruction: After the seizure stops, gently roll them into the recovery position (on their side). Check breathing.
steps.tip: They will be confused and drowsy. Speak calmly. Stay with them until fully alert.

steps.instruction: Call emergency services if: first seizure ever, seizure > 5 minutes, multiple seizures, injured during seizure, breathing difficulty after, or person is pregnant/diabetic.
steps.tip: Note what happened before, during, and after — paramedics will need this info.

when_to_stop: Seizure ends and person is breathing normally
do_not: Do NOT put anything in their mouth
Do NOT give food or water until fully alert
Do NOT move them unless in danger
